113595.explanation: For Algemeen Belang, it is not about gender but about quality.

113596.explanation: In exceptional cases, residents should be consulted. Algemeen Belang is fundamentally not in favor of referendums; for that, we go to the polls every four years.

113600.explanation: Algemeen Belang sees the municipality's role primarily in passing on funds from The Hague.

113602.explanation: Algemeen Belang is critical of the asylum policy from The Hague. We reject large-scale asylum seeker centers that shift the asylum task of multiple municipalities onto one of our towns. For establishing an asylum center, local support in the area is an absolute condition.

113607.explanation: Outside the fortress area, fireworks are part of New Year's Eve for Algemeen Belang. Within our fortress, the fireworks ban is a good solution. Shooting carbide must be preserved as cultural heritage.

113610.explanation: This is not a task of the (municipal) government. Making municipal buildings accessible is a task of the municipality.

113615.explanation: The music school receives a subsidy from the municipality of Elburg.

113616.explanation: Our associations are important places for meeting and caring for each other. Associations play a significant role within our community.

113626.explanation: Algemeen Belang wants to build in all towns. Infill development takes precedence over expansion.

113632.explanation: The area access roads and main roads such as the provincial road remain at 50 km/h.

113633.explanation: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

113634.explanation: It is about the good balance between price and quality.

113638.explanation: Algemeen Belang gives space to our local entrepreneurs. Algemeen Belang also works on housing construction.

113639.explanation: Algemeen Belang is a Christian party and does not support a 24/7 economy. A collective day of rest protects social and societal values. Moreover, Sunday openings put pressure on small entrepreneurs and favor large retail chains.

113641.explanation: Many waste streams are already free of charge. Costs may be charged for waste that cannot be reused.

113643.explanation: All neighborhood buildings and multi-functional accommodations are important for meeting, club life, and the livability of all our towns. This certainly applies to Oostendorp as well!

113645.explanation: Swimming lessons are arranged by parents in our country. Schools have no room for this in their full curriculum. For parents who cannot afford swimming lessons, financial support is available.

113647.explanation: Algemeen Belang wants to build for young and old, in all towns. Pre-fixing percentages does not help to realize those homes quickly. Building homes independently must also be possible.

113650.explanation: No additional parking spaces should be created in the inner city. For events, extra parking spaces should be created around the inner city.

113652.explanation: From 18 years old, you are warmly welcomed by Algemeen Belang in the real municipal council.

113655.explanation: CCTV surveillance should only be used in cases of vandalism and nuisance. So certainly not at every playground. Algemeen Belang recovers at least the costs of damage from the perpetrators.

113595.explanation: We strive for diversity and quality. Suitability and trust are leading, not a mandatory distribution.

113596.explanation: We value participation and consultation but find good representation and dialogue more important than binding referendums.

113600.explanation: We support residents who want to generate sustainable energy together, such as with shared solar roofs. We also look at smart solutions in the neighborhood.

113602.explanation: We take responsibility for shelter, but advocate for spreading and proper integration, not one fixed scenario.

113607.explanation: We wish the community a beautiful and safe end of the year with an organized fireworks show.

113610.explanation: We find it important that everyone can participate. The municipality can think along and advise, but the costs are for the entrepreneur themselves.

113615.explanation: Culture is valuable, but not everything can happen at once. Support must remain possible for families who need it.

113616.explanation: Sports clubs are important for meeting and health; the municipality must continue to actively contribute to thinking along with issues.

113619.explanation: We choose safety with a human dimension, not just more surveillance. We actively focus on supporting existing resident initiatives such as parent groups and neighborhood volunteers.

113626.explanation: We choose careful building, sometimes also on the edges of the towns.

113627.explanation: Participation is important, but always appropriate and voluntary. We believe it is good and desirable for everyone to contribute to society within their own possibilities.

113629.explanation: The municipality of Elburg has a generous minimum policy. We want to continuously consider the human dimension to prevent people from falling between the cracks.

113631.explanation: The municipality already does a lot regarding charging stations. We follow developments and adjust policy where necessary.

113632.explanation: The safety of our residents takes priority over everything.

113633.explanation: Freedom of belief and appropriate care are important.

113634.explanation: Quality and trust are paramount in youth care services.

113638.explanation: We support economic space, provided it is appropriate and careful. Not all businesses are suitable for the municipality of Elburg.

113639.explanation: We believe the economy should serve society, not the other way around. When everything must always be open, the human dimension comes under pressure. By setting limits on a 24-hour economy, we protect space for balance between work, care, and leisure. Rest is not a luxury, but a necessity.

113643.explanation: We find meeting places important for the residents of our communities. The community center is owned by the East Village Neighborhood Association; they maintain and manage the building. The municipality supports the neighborhood association with subsidies.

113645.explanation: Swimming safety is essential for children. For people with minimum income arrangements, we contribute to the costs of swimming lessons.

113647.explanation: Affordable housing is crucial for starters and families.

113649.explanation: We want to create living space quickly, and shared housing is a good response to this. We choose tailored solutions and protection of livability.

113650.explanation: There is sufficient parking available. Additionally, we encourage cycling among our own residents.

113652.explanation: Young people deserve a voice in policy.

113653.explanation: Recreation is important, but so are nature and tranquility.

113655.explanation: We choose social safety and supervision by people. Camera surveillance at playgrounds does not fit with this.

113595.explanation: It is nice if the representation reflects society, but LEV prefers to assess suitable candidates based on their competencies.

113596.explanation: In very exceptional cases this could be a solution, but councilors are primarily representatives of the people, so the mandate lies there first and foremost.

113600.explanation: LEV would like to see support provided, not necessarily financial, but we do not exclude that either. This concerns residents initially, so a revolving fund could provide a solution.

113602.explanation: Not suitable for the scale of our municipality. The municipality of Elburg does not need to become the 'problem owner' of issues that our national government has let get out of hand for years. LEV supports small-scale shelter with the aim of quick integration.

113607.explanation: Fine if the municipality wants to do this, but many residents of Elburg also enjoy their own fireworks display in a domestic setting.

113610.explanation: Why must this always be financial? That is possible, but the primary responsibility lies with the entrepreneur, who generally wants to be hospitable to everyone in our society.

113615.explanation: LEV agrees with a contribution to this development of the child, but it must be ensured that the child actually participates regularly in the music lessons.

113616.explanation: Not automatically. The financial policy of this sports association and the club’s viability must be examined. If all signals are green, LEV has no problem contributing.

113619.explanation: A difficult subject. Agreed in principle, but primarily to be able to enforce, because we are experiencing a crisis of authority in the Netherlands. LEV therefore wants no reinforcement on paper, but in practice. Not tough enforcement only on minor issues, but daring to intervene when truly necessary.

113626.explanation: That luxury is currently not available, but we must handle it wisely.

113627.explanation: Meaning and purpose are so important. We do this not to hinder people with benefits, but to look if they can gradually return to the labor process. Of course, not everyone with welfare benefits is capable of this.

113629.explanation: That could be a consideration; everyday life is becoming unaffordable, but social institutions must maintain contact with the Elburg Council on this as well.

113631.explanation: That very much depends on how the national Government demonstrates reliability. First encouraging electric driving, even financially, then taxing electric drivers, which again discourages electric driving.

113632.explanation: That is already the case in many residential neighborhoods. 30 km/h is too slow compared to the many two-wheelers, causing cars to be overtaken by fast two-wheelers.

113633.explanation: A caregiver is neutral and must especially remain so.

113634.explanation: That’s never wrong, but price alone doesn’t say everything about the care provided.

113638.explanation: Always do so when there is a direct necessity. A good economy is the foundation of a thriving municipality of Elburg.

113639.explanation: The freedom to do on Sunday whatever each resident/entrepreneur wishes will be one of our spearheads in the coming elections.

113643.explanation: Every community center reaches the moment for renewal at some point, so these are matters to discuss together.

113645.explanation: One of the basic principles to keep our youth safe.

113647.explanation: That has already been promoted several times from the Council, among others through motions and amendments.

113649.explanation: That is a solution, but we would like to know from landlords if there is a demand for this.

113650.explanation: This will be a difficult task in a monumental city.

113652.explanation: That would be fantastic, also to ultimately rejuvenate our Council, but LEV doubts there is immediate overwhelming enthusiasm for this. We like to be surprised and we are in favor of rejuvenation.

113653.explanation: Here you go again upfront with money. We do not exclude a financial contribution, but primarily it is something for the entrepreneurs. Besides, if Elburg remains closed on Sundays, then it is questionable whether entrepreneurs will be interested.

113655.explanation: Privacy, privacy, privacy! Elburg must promote physical supervision and act immediately where necessary.

113595.explanation: It is important that our municipal government is a good reflection of the Elburg population. This also means that the College of Mayor and Aldermen preferably consists of an equal number of men and women.

113596.explanation: Political decisions should not be made "about" residents, but as much as possible "together with" residents. Residents should also be able to give their opinion on issues that directly affect them.

113600.explanation: This is the ideal scenario: residents jointly generate their own green and affordable electricity. They use electricity in the same place, so the grid is not burdened. Energy cooperatives are an asset to our municipality and should be encouraged.

113602.explanation: The sheltering of people who have fled war and violence is a responsibility for all of us. We create space together for an asylum seekers' center (AZC) that fits the scale of Elburg. This is done in consultation and cooperation with residents and social partners such as associations, churches, and foundations.

113607.explanation: If we want people to stop setting off fireworks themselves, there must be a good alternative. A fireworks show organized by the municipality is a great way to usher in the new year safely and together.

113610.explanation: Accessibility is vital for people with disabilities. However, many important places are not yet properly accessible, for example for someone in a wheelchair. Entrepreneurs cannot simply afford such adjustments. Therefore, we believe the municipality should step in.

113615.explanation: Music lessons should be accessible to every child. It is a good outlet and fosters connection. We want music lessons to be accessible not only to children from wealthy families.

113616.explanation: Sports are a good outlet for many; it gives you energy and keeps you healthy. The sports club is also a meeting place. If a sports club collapses, the neighborhood suffers. Therefore, we believe the municipality should step in when needed.

113619.explanation: It is important that even in a small municipality like Elburg there are enough public order officers (BOA) to guarantee street safety. Besides enforcement, prevention is very important. We believe in engaging the local environment with the neighborhood as an ecosystem.

113626.explanation: Although new construction within existing village cores is preferred, it is inevitable that building will also occur outside existing boundaries. We will not build in nature.

113627.explanation: A social assistance benefit is a safety net. People without or with insufficient income should have the right to a benefit without being required to provide a counterperformance. They need a stable foundation and trust from the municipality. Additional conditions create more uncertainty.

113629.explanation: Too many low-income people run into financial problems. This can lead to debts but also broader issues like stress and health problems. Timely financial support is therefore important.

113631.explanation: One third of newly sold cars are electric. Even more cars are hybrid. The demand for charging facilities is increasing, and it is the municipality's task to provide for this.

113632.explanation: Driving slowly saves lives. A lower maximum speed within built-up areas is safer and better for the environment.

113633.explanation: In healthcare, it is also important to be attentive to people with different cultural or religious backgrounds. Healthcare professionals are trained for this. The healthcare labor market is under such pressure that a preferred choice is not always possible.

113634.explanation: High-quality youth care is more important than a low price. Especially because good care for young people who need it prevents major problems later in their lives.

113638.explanation: Elburg has several industrial estates; according to GroenLinks-PvdA, no expansion is needed there.

113639.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to make it possible for entrepreneurs to open their shops on Sundays as well.

113641.explanation: Litter on the street is unpleasant, unsafe, and unhealthy. It is good if residents dispose of their waste so the municipality can properly separate and process it. This results in clean streets and a healthy living environment. Therefore, we believe that the recycling center should be free for private individuals.

113643.explanation: It is important that residents of all villages within the municipality of Elburg have a suitable and pleasant meeting place.

113645.explanation: It is important that every child learns to swim at a young age. If school swimming can help with this, then it is important that the municipality provides funding.

113647.explanation: It is important that there are sufficient affordable homes for people with different incomes. This means the municipality should mainly invest in social rental housing. A minimum of one-third social rental housing in new construction projects is our starting point, alongside ensuring affordable starter homes.

113649.explanation: There is increasing demand for small-scale housing, for example for single individuals. More flexible rules for renting out single rooms can help with this.

113650.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA supports an almost car-free center of Elburg. Parking can be arranged on various sites outside the center.

113652.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA finds it important that everyone can participate, including in local politics. It should also be possible for young people to think about social issues at an early stage.

113653.explanation: The groyne is a beautiful area that we want to preserve as it is. It offers a piece of free nature experience in harmony with the activities of the associations present there. This has been priceless for many generations of children.

113655.explanation: The use of cameras in public spaces should be kept to a minimum. Supervision by residents, including parents and caregivers, is the best way to prevent nuisance.

113595.explanation: The SGP believes that administrative functions should be filled based on suitability and trust. Quality, responsibility, and support must be decisive, not a pre-imposed distribution between men and women.

113596.explanation: The SGP supports a representative democracy of elected representatives who jointly, as a council, determine the policy of our municipality. Policy requires careful consideration; therefore, in our view, a referendum that reduces an issue to a simple 'yes' or 'no' is not appropriate.

113600.explanation: The municipality offers attractive so-called sustainability loans. This is a means to help residents take steps in generating sustainable energy. Purchase and investment remain the responsibility of the resident.

113602.explanation: We believe that as the municipality of Elburg, we also have a responsibility regarding the reception of asylum seekers. However, this does not necessarily mean that an asylum seekers’ center must be established in Elburg by definition.

113607.explanation: The SGP believes that organizing a fireworks show is not a task for the municipality. The municipality can contribute to a good New Year's Eve in other ways, but organizing a fireworks show does not fit that role.

113610.explanation: The municipality can facilitate, but the building is primarily the entrepreneur's responsibility. This also applies to making the building accessible to people with disabilities.

113615.explanation: Playing an instrument is good for a child's development. To stimulate this and potentially remove financial barriers, it is good that every primary school child gets the chance to take music lessons.

113616.explanation: The SGP does not see it as the government's task to keep sports clubs afloat. If membership numbers decline, it is the club's responsibility to turn things around, with the municipality providing facilitation as appropriate.

113619.explanation: The SGP considers safety a core task of the government. Visible and sufficient enforcement is necessary to uphold rules, prevent nuisance, and offer residents a safe living environment.

113626.explanation: We must make the most of housing opportunities within the villages. Subsequently, building according to the "just another street" principle should follow, and if necessary, village boundaries can be extended. Outside the village boundaries, in the rural area, we primarily prefer housing on existing plots.

113627.explanation: The SGP finds it natural that those who can contribute, do so. A counterperformance when receiving benefits promotes participation, self-esteem, and involvement in society.

113629.explanation: At present, we have generous minimum social policies in the municipality of Elburg. Residents who are truly in need should be helped. In addition, we believe it is important that society also plays its role. Think of churches, associations, but also neighborhoods looking out for each other.

113631.explanation: The SGP sees no direct signs that there is currently a serious shortage of charging points in the municipality of Elburg. If the need increases, it is good for the municipality to designate more places for this, which generally should not come at the expense of necessary parking spaces for non-electric cars.

113632.explanation: In many places within built-up areas, 30 km/h already applies. If lowering the current 50 km/h speed limit to 30 km/h improves traffic safety, then we should do so. However, a blanket 30 km/h everywhere within built-up areas is not our position.

113633.explanation: The SGP believes that people have the right to care that aligns with their convictions and beliefs. Freedom of choice in healthcare is essential, especially for vulnerable residents who depend on support.

113634.explanation: Of course, we are aware that the costs for youth care for the municipality are very high. Nevertheless, focusing only on price can be counterproductive. A good alignment of the organization with the world of our youth can ultimately make care trajectories more effective and thereby reduce costs.

113638.explanation: The SGP believes there must be room for local businesses. Expanding industrial estates is responsible when it fits the scale of the municipality and contributes to employment for local residents.

113639.explanation: The SGP values Sunday as a collective day of rest. Society has increasingly craved peace in recent years. Shopping Sundays undermine rest, family and community life, and therefore do not fit with a healthy society.

113641.explanation: Some waste streams can already be offered free of charge as part of the introduction of Diftar. That must remain so. The processing costs of bulky waste are such that free acceptance is, in our view, not an option.

113643.explanation: The SGP currently does not consider it wise to renew the community center. First, we want clarity about the housing plans behind the town hall. Possibly that will create room for a better and future-proof solution.

113645.explanation: The SGP considers swimming proficiency essential to the safety of children. School swimming prevents financial circumstances of a family from determining whether a child learns to swim.

113647.explanation: The SGP believes there must be enough affordable housing for people with lower incomes. A substantial share of social rental housing is necessary to offer all residents perspective on housing.

113649.explanation: The SGP sees house sharing and splitting as a way to alleviate the housing shortage. This can, if applied carefully, contribute to affordable living without undermining livability.

113650.explanation: The SGP considers good parking facilities in the center important. Additional parking spaces are desirable, but only if spatially and traffic-wise feasible and do not detract from the historical appearance.

113652.explanation: The SGP has a heart for young people and actively involves them in politics and society. Youth participation is important, but in the SGP’s view, a separate youth council is not the right means for this.

113653.explanation: The Strekdam is a unique part of Elburg that, in the SGP’s opinion, should be left untouched.

113655.explanation: The SGP believes playgrounds must be safe and pleasant. Camera surveillance can help prevent vandalism and nuisance, allowing children to play without worries. Playgrounds have proven to be hotspots for nuisance in recent years.

113595.explanation: Women must have full participation in our municipal government. Therefore, if possible, an equal distribution of men and women is a good starting point.

113596.explanation: Balancing interests on important matters takes place in the municipal council, which already reflects society. Councilors are ultimately elected representatives of the people.

113600.explanation: Residents can expect support from the municipality, but that does not always have to be financial aid. The municipality can also help with faster permit issuance, so such initiatives remain feasible. Not everything can be funded by the municipality.

113602.explanation: We take our responsibility for humane reception according to the distribution law. Together with the region we work on the required accommodation, linked to good provisions for care and education.

113607.explanation: A fireworks show is not the municipality’s responsibility by default. An initiative from society can count on our support.

113610.explanation: Everyone must be able to participate, including people with disabilities. We want accessible spaces and safe sidewalks.

113615.explanation: We invest in equal opportunities and the broad development of children. Parents who cannot afford the fees can apply for assistance through the child package.

113616.explanation: Associations are indispensable for solidarity and community. Financial support from the municipality is for the short term, so associations can stand on their own feet as quickly as possible.

113619.explanation: The police cannot solve all municipal problems. These tasks are shifting to municipal enforcement. Expansion is good for safety in Elburg.

113626.explanation: For now, Elburg can meet its housing needs without harming open countryside. Until then, preserving the open countryside is very important to us. Should the situation arise, expansion along the village edges is not off the table.

113627.explanation: Where possible, this is desirable: suitable work or daytime activities. Everyone has their own responsibility. Offering opportunities for social work helps people participate again in society.

113629.explanation: We invest in equal opportunities by providing extra support for children and adults living in poverty.

113631.explanation: The CDA wants safe, accessible, and sustainable mobility for everyone. We want to encourage the use of electric cars (and bicycles).

113632.explanation: Traffic safety for cyclists is our priority.

113633.explanation: People must be able to receive care that aligns with their religion, culture or beliefs. The municipality of Elburg must support this freedom of choice, within quality requirements and available care.

113634.explanation: The quality of youth care must always come first. Price is only one of many evaluation criteria.

113638.explanation: Our entrepreneurs need workspaces. Expanding industrial estates is necessary for that.

113639.explanation: Sunday in our municipality is collectively dedicated to personal relaxation, reflection and meeting others. It is important to us that everyone may decide for themselves how they want to observe this, providing room for the diversity in our municipality.

113641.explanation: Some waste streams can already be disposed of free of charge. It is financially not feasible to make all waste streams free.

113643.explanation: Renewing the Eastendorp community center strengthens livability and social interaction in Eastendorp. The municipality of Elburg thus invests in cohesion, volunteer work and caring for one another. Of course, the renewal must be feasible.

113645.explanation: School swimming is not the solution to ensure children obtain their swimming diploma.

113647.explanation: In new construction plans, at least 40% of the homes remain designated for senior housing: small-scale neighborhood structures (courtyards, residential yards) with social rent, affordable ownership and reasonably priced homeownership.

113649.explanation: Offering single rooms can be a solution for the housing shortage, especially for young people and students.

113650.explanation: We see that the parking spaces do not meet the demand, such as during events and sports occasions.

113652.explanation: Of course it is important to involve young people in local politics. A youth council is not the only solution for that.

113653.explanation: We want more multi-day recreational stays, extension of the season, and more water sports related developments. The Strekdam is a forgotten area with great potential.

113655.explanation: In principle, we see no need for camera surveillance, but in some cases it can help to counter problems (loitering youth, nuisance). The CDA is not against temporary supervision in such cases.

statements.title: A youth council should be established for young people aged 12 to 20.

titleParts.glossary: A group of young people who provide input and discuss plans and policy with the municipality.

titleParts.text: komen voor jongeren tussen de 12 en 20 jaar.

statements.title: Elburg should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: Elburg should invest money in developing water sports, campsites and holiday parks on the Strekdam.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: In Elburg, the business parks may be expanded.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: Elburg should renovate the Buurtgebouw Oostendorp community building.
The municipality must not allow Sunday shopping.
More parking spaces should be created in the centre of Elburg.
Dropping off waste at the recycling center should be free of charge.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: Residents of Elburg should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Elburg moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: At least 40% of the newly built homes in Elburg should be social rental housing. In the Netherlands, 28% of homes are currently social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Elburg moet minimaal 40% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: There should be video surveillance at the playgrounds in Elburg.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

titleParts.text: komen bij de speeltuinen in Elburg.
